ignorePatterns:
greptile.json
*.lock
package-lock.json
yarn.lock
pnpm-lock.yaml
**/*.generated.*
**/node_modules/**
**/.next/**
**/dist/**
**/build/**
**/.git/**
**/*.log
.tracking-db/**
docs/sessions/**
go/dist/**
**/.claude/agent-memory/**
**/.claude/agent-memory-local/**
**/.sigma/backlogs/**
**/.sigma/orchestration/**
.sprint-state.json
.pipeline-state.json
*.min.js
*.min.css
*.map
instructions: This codebase follows the Sigma Protocol methodology. Focus on: (1) Logic bugs and edge cases, (2) Security vulnerabilities (hardcoded secrets, missing auth checks, injection risks), (3) Anti-slop patterns (verbose comments that restate code, hedging language in error messages, unnecessary filler), (4) Missing error handling at system boundaries, (5) Type safety gaps. Do NOT flag: minor style preferences already handled by linters, TODO comments (these are intentional), test file structure choices, import ordering.

rules.rule: Flag any hardcoded API keys, tokens, passwords, or secrets. Look for patterns: sk-, ghp_, gho_, AKIA, password=, secret=, api_key=, private_key, BEGIN RSA PRIVATE. Environment variables or vault references are the correct approach.
Every user-facing API endpoint or mutation must verify authentication before processing. Flag endpoints that skip auth verification.

Flag verbose comments that restate what the code already says. Comments like '// This function does X' where X is obvious from the function name, '// Initialize variables', '// Return the result', or separator comments like '// ---' and '// ===' are noise. Comments should explain WHY, not WHAT.
Flag console.log, console.warn, console.error in production code. Use a structured logger instead. Exception: test files and dev-only debug utilities.
Async functions that call external APIs, databases, or file system must have error handling (try-catch or .catch). Internal function calls between modules do not need defensive error handling.
Flag usage of 'any' type in TypeScript. Use 'unknown' with type narrowing, or define proper types. Exception: third-party library interop where types are unavailable.
User input from API requests, form submissions, and URL parameters must be validated before use. Use Zod schemas, Convex validators (v.*), or equivalent. Flag raw req.body access without validation.
Do not use barrel exports (index.ts that re-exports from other files). Import directly from source files. This prevents circular dependencies and improves tree-shaking.
